obligation_text: When implementing the risk management system, providers of high-risk AI systems shall give consideration to whether the high-risk AI system is likely to adversely impact persons under the age of 18 or, as appropriate, other vulnerable groups.
scoring_text: Knowledge of vulnerability assessment for AI systems, including methods for evaluating adverse impacts on minors and other vulnerable groups, and integration of vulnerability considerations into risk management processes.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of vulnerability assessment for AI systems, including methods for evaluating adverse impacts on minors and other vulnerable groups, and integration of vulnerability considerations into risk management processes.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identification, bias evaluation, human oversight documentation, and residual risk determination.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 9(8) (competency) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-005 is the strongest match (STS: 0.716, cosine: 0.684). Secondary: RM-S-002 (RM, cosine: 0.660), SP-S-008 (SP, cosine: 0.659).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.2/10 (raw 0.716). Shared vocabulary: [ai, risk]. EU AI Act competency requirement terms [adverse, assessment, considerations, evaluating, groups, impacts] vs WIDAI terms [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.890 (P=0.887, R=0.892). Balanced P/R — the 28-word EU AI Act competency and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.1%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.684 ≈ STS 0.716 (gap 0.031). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, risk] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.3%. Shared tokens: [ai, risk]. EU AI Act-only: [adverse, assessment, considerations, evaluating, groups, impacts]. WIDAI-only: [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation].
